<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Slang (astrologie)]][[als:Jahr der Schlange]][[ar:برج الثعبان]][[arz:تعبان (برج)]][[ast:Signu de la culiebra]][[ban:Lelipi (shio)]][[bcl:Halas (sodyak)]][[cdo:Sê̤ṳ (dê-ciĕ)]][[commons:Category:Snake (zodiac)]][[en:Snake (zodiac)]][[es:Signo de la serpiente]][[et:Maoaasta]][[fa:مار (منطقةالبروج)]][[fi:Käärme (kiinalainen horoskooppi)]][[fr:Serpent (astrologie chinoise)]][[hak:Sṳ (thi-chṳ̂)]][[id:Ular (shio)]][[it:Serpente (zodiaco cinese)]][[jv:Ula (shio)]][[ko:사 (지지)]][[lt:Gyvatė (kinų zodiakas)]][[mg:Bibilava (zôdiaka sinoa)]][[ms:Ular (zodiak)]][[nl:Slang (astrologie)]][[pt:Serpente (zodíaco)]][[simple:Snake (zodiac)]][[sv:Orm (zodiak)]][[ta:பாம்பு (சீன சோதிடம்)]][[th:มะเส็ง]][[tl:Ahas (sodyak)]][[tr:Yılan (burç)]][[uk:Змія (зодіак)]][[vi:Tỵ]][[war:Halas (zodyak)]][[za:Ceih (deihcei)]] {{Short description|Sign of the Chinese zodiac}} {{About|the [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] sign||Snake (disambiguation)}} {{redirect|Year of the Snake|the album by the group Fly|Year of the Snake (album)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2018}} {{infobox Chinese |pic=Snake (Chinese characters).svg |piccap="Snake" in [[regular script|regular]] Chinese characters |picupright=0.35 |c=蛇 |p=shé |w=shê<sup>2</sup> |mi={{IPAc-cmn|sh|e|2}} |j=se4 |y=sèh |ci={{IPAc-yue|s|e|4}} |h=sà |poj=chôa |oc-b92=*{{IPA|ljAj}} |oc-bs=*{{IPA|Cə.lAj}} }} [[File:OMBRE CHINOISE SERPENT.jpg|thumb|Zodiac snake, showing the ''shé'' (蛇) character for snake]] The '''snake''' ('''''') is the sixth of the twelve-year cycle of [[:en:Animal|Animal]]s which appear in the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] related to the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>
==Gallery== Depictions of zodiacal snakes, alone or with the other eleven signs, show how they have been imagined in the calendrical context. <gallery> File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle.JPG|[[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]] [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] circle: a stone circle inscribed with [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]]s of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] near the entrance to the [[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]], [[:en:Yunnan|Yunnan]] File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle detail.JPG|Detail of above, showing the Snake designated by its [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] sign (front, center right) File:Cernuschi Museum 20060812 135.jpg|[[:en:Terracotta|Terracotta]] [[:en:Astrological sign|zodiacal]] Snake from the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] (581-618). File:KCityParkSnake.JPG|The Snake statue is one of the 12 [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] portrayed in the [[:en:Kowloon Walled City|Kowloon Walled City Park]] in [[:en:Kowloon City|Kowloon City]], [[:en:Hong Kong|Hong Kong]] </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Slang (astrologie)]][[als:Jahr der Schlange]][[ar:برج الثعبان]][[arz:تعبان (برج)]][[ast:Signu de la culiebra]][[ban:Lelipi (shio)]][[bcl:Halas (sodyak)]][[cdo:Sê̤ṳ (dê-ciĕ)]][[sn:commons:Category:Snake (zodiac)]][[en:Snake (zodiac)]][[es:Signo de la serpiente]][[et:Maoaasta]][[fa:مار (منطقةالبروج)]][[fi:Käärme (kiinalainen horoskooppi)]][[fr:Serpent (astrologie chinoise)]][[hak:Sṳ (thi-chṳ̂)]][[id:Ular (shio)]][[it:Serpente (zodiaco cinese)]][[jv:Ula (shio)]][[ko:사 (지지)]][[lt:Gyvatė (kinų zodiakas)]][[mg:Bibilava (zôdiaka sinoa)]][[ms:Ular (zodiak)]][[nl:Slang (astrologie)]][[pt:Serpente (zodíaco)]][[simple:Snake (zodiac)]][[sv:Orm (zodiak)]][[ta:பாம்பு (சீன சோதிடம்)]][[th:มะเส็ง]][[tl:Ahas (sodyak)]][[tr:Yılan (burç)]][[uk:Змія (зодіак)]][[vi:Tỵ]][[war:Halas (zodyak)]][[za:Ceih (deihcei)]] {{Short description|Sign of the Chinese zodiac}} {{About|the [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] sign||Snake (disambiguation)}} {{redirect|Year of the Snake|the album by the group Fly|Year of the Snake (album)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2018}} {{infobox Chinese |pic=Snake (Chinese characters).svg |piccap="Snake" in [[regular script|regular]] Chinese characters |picupright=0.35 |c=蛇 |p=shé |w=shê<sup>2</sup> |mi={{IPAc-cmn|sh|e|2}} |j=se4 |y=sèh |ci={{IPAc-yue|s|e|4}} |h=sà |poj=chôa |oc-b92=*{{IPA|ljAj}} |oc-bs=*{{IPA|Cə.lAj}} }} [[File:OMBRE CHINOISE SERPENT.jpg|thumb|Zodiac snake, showing the ''shé'' (蛇) character for snake]] The '''snake''' ('''''') is the sixth of the twelve-year cycle of [[:en:Animal|Animal]]s which appear in the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] related to the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". The following table lists dates for years of the snake together with the associated elemental signs: {| class="wikitable" |- ! Start date !! End date !! In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>
==Gallery== Depictions of zodiacal snakes, alone or with the other eleven signs, show how they have been imagined in the calendrical context. <gallery> File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle.JPG|[[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]] [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] circle: a stone circle inscribed with [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]]s of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] near the entrance to the [[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]], [[:en:Yunnan|Yunnan]] File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle detail.JPG|Detail of above, showing the Snake designated by its [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] sign (front, center right) File:Cernuschi Museum 20060812 135.jpg|[[:en:Terracotta|Terracotta]] [[:en:Astrological sign|zodiacal]] Snake from the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] (581-618). File:KCityParkSnake.JPG|The Snake statue is one of the 12 [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] portrayed in the [[:en:Kowloon Walled City|Kowloon Walled City Park]] in [[:en:Kowloon City|Kowloon City]], [[:en:Hong Kong|Hong Kong]] </gallery>

In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>
==Gallery== Depictions of zodiacal snakes, alone or with the other eleven signs, show how they have been imagined in the calendrical context. <gallery> File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle.JPG|[[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]] [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] circle: a stone circle inscribed with [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]]s of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] near the entrance to the [[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]], [[:en:Yunnan|Yunnan]] File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle detail.JPG|Detail of above, showing the Snake designated by its [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] sign (front, center right) File:Cernuschi Museum 20060812 135.jpg|[[:en:Terracotta|Terracotta]] [[:en:Astrological sign|zodiacal]] Snake from the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] (581-618). File:KCityParkSnake.JPG|The Snake statue is one of the 12 [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] portrayed in the [[:en:Kowloon Walled City|Kowloon Walled City Park]] in [[:en:Kowloon City|Kowloon City]], [[:en:Hong Kong|Hong Kong]] </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Slang (astrologie)]][[als:Jahr der Schlange]][[ar:برج الثعبان]][[arz:تعبان (برج)]][[ast:Signu de la culiebra]][[ban:Lelipi (shio)]][[bcl:Halas (sodyak)]][[cdo:Sê̤ṳ (dê-ciĕ)]][[sn:commons:Category:Snake (zodiac)]][[en:Snake (zodiac)]][[es:Signo de la serpiente]][[et:Maoaasta]][[fa:مار (منطقةالبروج)]][[fi:Käärme (kiinalainen horoskooppi)]][[fr:Serpent (astrologie chinoise)]][[hak:Sṳ (thi-chṳ̂)]][[id:Ular (shio)]][[it:Serpente (zodiaco cinese)]][[jv:Ula (shio)]][[ko:사 (지지)]][[lt:Gyvatė (kinų zodiakas)]][[mg:Bibilava (zôdiaka sinoa)]][[ms:Ular (zodiak)]][[nl:Slang (astrologie)]][[pt:Serpente (zodíaco)]][[simple:Snake (zodiac)]][[sv:Orm (zodiak)]][[ta:பாம்பு (சீன சோதிடம்)]][[th:มะเส็ง]][[tl:Ahas (sodyak)]][[tr:Yılan (burç)]][[uk:Змія (зодіак)]][[vi:Tỵ]][[war:Halas (zodyak)]][[za:Ceih (deihcei)]] {{Short description|Sign of the Chinese zodiac}} {{About|the [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] sign||Snake (disambiguation)}} {{redirect|Year of the Snake|the album by the group Fly|Year of the Snake (album)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2018}} {{infobox Chinese |pic=Snake (Chinese characters).svg |piccap="Snake" in [[regular script|regular]] Chinese characters |picupright=0.35 |c=蛇 |p=shé |w=shê<sup>2</sup> |mi={{IPAc-cmn|sh|e|2}} |j=se4 |y=sèh |ci={{IPAc-yue|s|e|4}} |h=sà |poj=chôa |oc-b92=*{{IPA|ljAj}} |oc-bs=*{{IPA|Cə.lAj}} }} [[File:OMBRE CHINOISE SERPENT.jpg|thumb|Zodiac snake, showing the ''shé'' (蛇) character for snake]] The '''snake''' ('''''') is the sixth of the twelve-year cycle of [[:en:Animal|Animal]]s which appear in the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] related to the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>
==Gallery== Depictions of zodiacal snakes, alone or with the other eleven signs, show how they have been imagined in the calendrical context. <gallery> File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle.JPG|[[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]] [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] circle: a stone circle inscribed with [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]]s of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] near the entrance to the [[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]], [[:en:Yunnan|Yunnan]] File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle detail.JPG|Detail of above, showing the Snake designated by its [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] sign (front, center right) File:Cernuschi Museum 20060812 135.jpg|[[:en:Terracotta|Terracotta]] [[:en:Astrological sign|zodiacal]] Snake from the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] (581-618). File:KCityParkSnake.JPG|The Snake statue is one of the 12 [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] portrayed in the [[:en:Kowloon Walled City|Kowloon Walled City Park]] in [[:en:Kowloon City|Kowloon City]], [[:en:Hong Kong|Hong Kong]] </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Slang (astrologie)]][[als:Jahr der Schlange]][[ar:برج الثعبان]][[arz:تعبان (برج)]][[ast:Signu de la culiebra]][[ban:Lelipi (shio)]][[bcl:Halas (sodyak)]][[cdo:Sê̤ṳ (dê-ciĕ)]][[sn:commons:Category:Snake (zodiac)]][[en:Snake (zodiac)]][[es:Signo de la serpiente]][[et:Maoaasta]][[fa:مار (منطقةالبروج)]][[fi:Käärme (kiinalainen horoskooppi)]][[fr:Serpent (astrologie chinoise)]][[hak:Sṳ (thi-chṳ̂)]][[id:Ular (shio)]][[it:Serpente (zodiaco cinese)]][[jv:Ula (shio)]][[ko:사 (지지)]][[lt:Gyvatė (kinų zodiakas)]][[mg:Bibilava (zôdiaka sinoa)]][[ms:Ular (zodiak)]][[nl:Slang (astrologie)]][[pt:Serpente (zodíaco)]][[simple:Snake (zodiac)]][[sv:Orm (zodiak)]][[ta:பாம்பு (சீன சோதிடம்)]][[th:มะเส็ง]][[tl:Ahas (sodyak)]][[tr:Yılan (burç)]][[uk:Змія (зодіак)]][[vi:Tỵ]][[war:Halas (zodyak)]][[za:Ceih (deihcei)]] {{Short description|Sign of the Chinese zodiac}} {{About|the [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] sign||Snake (disambiguation)}} {{redirect|Year of the Snake|the album by the group Fly|Year of the Snake (album)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2018}} {{infobox Chinese |pic=Snake (Chinese characters).svg |piccap="Snake" in [[regular script|regular]] Chinese characters |picupright=0.35 |c=蛇 |p=shé |w=shê<sup>2</sup> |mi={{IPAc-cmn|sh|e|2}} |j=se4 |y=sèh |ci={{IPAc-yue|s|e|4}} |h=sà |poj=chôa |oc-b92=*{{IPA|ljAj}} |oc-bs=*{{IPA|Cə.lAj}} }} [[File:OMBRE CHINOISE SERPENT.jpg|thumb|Zodiac snake, showing the ''shé'' (蛇) character for snake]] The '''snake''' ('''''') is the sixth of the twelve-year cycle of [[:en:Animal|Animal]]s which appear in the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] related to the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". == Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Slang (astrologie)]][[als:Jahr der Schlange]][[ar:برج الثعبان]][[arz:تعبان (برج)]][[ast:Signu de la culiebra]][[ban:Lelipi (shio)]][[bcl:Halas (sodyak)]][[cdo:Sê̤ṳ (dê-ciĕ)]][[sn:commons:Category:Snake (zodiac)]][[en:Snake (zodiac)]][[es:Signo de la serpiente]][[et:Maoaasta]][[fa:مار (منطقةالبروج)]][[fi:Käärme (kiinalainen horoskooppi)]][[fr:Serpent (astrologie chinoise)]][[hak:Sṳ (thi-chṳ̂)]][[id:Ular (shio)]][[it:Serpente (zodiaco cinese)]][[jv:Ula (shio)]][[ko:사 (지지)]][[lt:Gyvatė (kinų zodiakas)]][[mg:Bibilava (zôdiaka sinoa)]][[ms:Ular (zodiak)]][[nl:Slang (astrologie)]][[pt:Serpente (zodíaco)]][[simple:Snake (zodiac)]][[sv:Orm (zodiak)]][[ta:பாம்பு (சீன சோதிடம்)]][[th:มะเส็ง]][[tl:Ahas (sodyak)]][[tr:Yılan (burç)]][[uk:Змія (зодіак)]][[vi:Tỵ]][[war:Halas (zodyak)]][[za:Ceih (deihcei)]] {{Short description|Sign of the Chinese zodiac}} {{About|the [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] sign||Snake (disambiguation)}} {{redirect|Year of the Snake|the album by the group Fly|Year of the Snake (album)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2018}} {{infobox Chinese |pic=Snake (Chinese characters).svg |piccap="Snake" in [[regular script|regular]] Chinese characters |picupright=0.35 |c=蛇 |p=shé |w=shê<sup>2</sup> |mi={{IPAc-cmn|sh|e|2}} |j=se4 |y=sèh |ci={{IPAc-yue|s|e|4}} |h=sà |poj=chôa |oc-b92=*{{IPA|ljAj}} |oc-bs=*{{IPA|Cə.lAj}} }} [[File:OMBRE CHINOISE SERPENT.jpg|thumb|Zodiac snake, showing the ''shé'' (蛇) character for snake]] The '''snake''' ('''''') is the sixth of the twelve-year cycle of [[:en:Animal|Animal]]s which appear in the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] related to the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>

The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>

The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>

The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Slang (astrologie)]][[als:Jahr der Schlange]][[ar:برج الثعبان]][[arz:تعبان (برج)]][[ast:Signu de la culiebra]][[ban:Lelipi (shio)]][[bcl:Halas (sodyak)]][[cdo:Sê̤ṳ (dê-ciĕ)]][[sn:commons:Category:Snake (zodiac)]][[en:Snake (zodiac)]][[es:Signo de la serpiente]][[et:Maoaasta]][[fa:مار (منطقةالبروج)]][[fi:Käärme (kiinalainen horoskooppi)]][[fr:Serpent (astrologie chinoise)]][[hak:Sṳ (thi-chṳ̂)]][[id:Ular (shio)]][[it:Serpente (zodiaco cinese)]][[jv:Ula (shio)]][[ko:사 (지지)]][[lt:Gyvatė (kinų zodiakas)]][[mg:Bibilava (zôdiaka sinoa)]][[ms:Ular (zodiak)]][[nl:Slang (astrologie)]][[pt:Serpente (zodíaco)]][[simple:Snake (zodiac)]][[sv:Orm (zodiak)]][[ta:பாம்பு (சீன சோதிடம்)]][[th:มะเส็ง]][[tl:Ahas (sodyak)]][[tr:Yılan (burç)]][[uk:Змія (зодіак)]][[vi:Tỵ]][[war:Halas (zodyak)]][[za:Ceih (deihcei)]] {{Short description|Sign of the Chinese zodiac}} {{About|the [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] sign||Snake (disambiguation)}} {{redirect|Year of the Snake|the album by the group Fly|Year of the Snake (album)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2018}} {{infobox Chinese |pic=Snake (Chinese characters).svg |piccap="Snake" in [[regular script|regular]] Chinese characters |picupright=0.35 |c=蛇 |p=shé |w=shê<sup>2</sup> |mi={{IPAc-cmn|sh|e|2}} |j=se4 |y=sèh |ci={{IPAc-yue|s|e|4}} |h=sà |poj=chôa |oc-b92=*{{IPA|ljAj}} |oc-bs=*{{IPA|Cə.lAj}} }} [[File:OMBRE CHINOISE SERPENT.jpg|thumb|Zodiac snake, showing the ''shé'' (蛇) character for snake]] The '''snake''' ('''''') is the sixth of the twelve-year cycle of [[:en:Animal|Animal]]s which appear in the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] related to the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". The following table lists dates for years of the snake together with the associated elemental signs: {| class="wikitable" |- ! Start date !! End date !! In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.

The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Slang (astrologie)]][[als:Jahr der Schlange]][[ar:برج الثعبان]][[arz:تعبان (برج)]][[ast:Signu de la culiebra]][[ban:Lelipi (shio)]][[bcl:Halas (sodyak)]][[cdo:Sê̤ṳ (dê-ciĕ)]][[sn:commons:Category:Snake (zodiac)]][[en:Snake (zodiac)]][[es:Signo de la serpiente]][[et:Maoaasta]][[fa:مار (منطقةالبروج)]][[fi:Käärme (kiinalainen horoskooppi)]][[fr:Serpent (astrologie chinoise)]][[hak:Sṳ (thi-chṳ̂)]][[id:Ular (shio)]][[it:Serpente (zodiaco cinese)]][[jv:Ula (shio)]][[ko:사 (지지)]][[lt:Gyvatė (kinų zodiakas)]][[mg:Bibilava (zôdiaka sinoa)]][[ms:Ular (zodiak)]][[nl:Slang (astrologie)]][[pt:Serpente (zodíaco)]][[simple:Snake (zodiac)]][[sv:Orm (zodiak)]][[ta:பாம்பு (சீன சோதிடம்)]][[th:มะเส็ง]][[tl:Ahas (sodyak)]][[tr:Yılan (burç)]][[uk:Змія (зодіак)]][[vi:Tỵ]][[war:Halas (zodyak)]][[za:Ceih (deihcei)]] {{Short description|Sign of the Chinese zodiac}} {{About|the [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] sign||Snake (disambiguation)}} {{redirect|Year of the Snake|the album by the group Fly|Year of the Snake (album)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2018}} {{infobox Chinese |pic=Snake (Chinese characters).svg |piccap="Snake" in [[regular script|regular]] Chinese characters |picupright=0.35 |c=蛇 |p=shé |w=shê<sup>2</sup> |mi={{IPAc-cmn|sh|e|2}} |j=se4 |y=sèh |ci={{IPAc-yue|s|e|4}} |h=sà |poj=chôa |oc-b92=*{{IPA|ljAj}} |oc-bs=*{{IPA|Cə.lAj}} }} [[File:OMBRE CHINOISE SERPENT.jpg|thumb|Zodiac snake, showing the ''shé'' (蛇) character for snake]] The '''snake''' ('''''') is the sixth of the twelve-year cycle of [[:en:Animal|Animal]]s which appear in the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] related to the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>
==Gallery== Depictions of zodiacal snakes, alone or with the other eleven signs, show how they have been imagined in the calendrical context. <gallery> File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle.JPG|[[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]] [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]] circle: a stone circle inscribed with [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]]s of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] near the entrance to the [[:en:Old Town of Lijiang|Old Town of Lijiang]], [[:en:Yunnan|Yunnan]] File:Old Town of Lijiang UNESCO zodiac circle detail.JPG|Detail of above, showing the Snake designated by its [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] sign (front, center right) File:Cernuschi Museum 20060812 135.jpg|[[:en:Terracotta|Terracotta]] [[:en:Astrological sign|zodiacal]] Snake from the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]] (581-618). File:KCityParkSnake.JPG|The Snake statue is one of the 12 [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] portrayed in the [[:en:Kowloon Walled City|Kowloon Walled City Park]] in [[:en:Kowloon City|Kowloon City]], [[:en:Hong Kong|Hong Kong]] </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:Snakes in Chinese mythology|Snakes in Chinese mythology]] *[[:en:Snakes in mythology|Snakes in mythology]] *[[:en:Serpent (symbolism)|Serpent (symbolism)]]

In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the new sign of the zodiac starts on 1 January, while in [[:en:China|China]] it starts, according to the traditional Chinese calendar, at the [[:en:New moon|New moon]] that falls between 21 January and 20 February, so that persons born in January or February may have two different signs in the two countries, but persons born in late February (i.e. on or after 20 February) automatically have one sign in both countries.
==Basic astrological associations== {{See also|Yin and yang}} [[:en:Astrology|Astrology]] is a cross-cultural phenomenon which remains popular in many cultures or subcultures. For whatever reason, never seeming to lose popularity, modern trends include a tendency towards [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]], in which various beliefs of different origin are compounded together. The following table gives examples of this, with such popular ideas as lucky numbers ([[:en:Numerology|Numerology]]), lucky colors and lucky times (general aspects of the idea of [[:en:en:Luck|en:Luck]]), together with the suggestions toward choosing a suitable partner for an [[:en:intimate relationship|intimate relationship]], [[:en:Marriage|Marriage]], or [[:en:Business|Business]], for people born during a snake year or generally for the year.
{| class="wikitable" |- |Earthly branch:|| Sì |- |Element:|| [[:en:Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|Fire]] |- |Planet:|| [[:en:Venus|Venus]] |- |Yin Yang:|| Yīn |- |Lunar month:|| Fourth |- |Lucky numbers:|| 2, 8, 9 |- |Lucky flowers:|| [[:en:Orchidaceae|Orchid]], [[:en:cactus|cactus]] |- |Lucky colors:|| Red, light yellow, black; Avoid: white, golden, brown<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.yourchineseastrology.com/zodiac/snake.htm | title=Chinese Zodiac – Snake | publisher=Your Chinese Astrology | access-date=14 March 2018}}</ref> |- |Season:|| Summer |- |}
The snake is the sixth of the twelve signs and belongs to the second trine, with the '''[[Ox (zodiac)|ox]]''' (second sign, 牛, Earthly branch: 丑) and the '''[[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|rooster]]''' (tenth sign, {{Lang-zh|p=jī|s=鸡|t=雞/鷄|labels=no|first=t}}, Earthly branch: 酉), with which it is most compatible. The pig is the most incompatible.<ref>[https://vansu.net/lich-am-duong.html Vansu Calendar], 23 January 2023</ref>
== Cultural notes == A Snake Year is sometimes referred to as a Little Dragon Year to assuage possible feelings of inadequacy among people born during a Snake Year.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Lary |first=Diana |url= |title=China's grandmothers : gender, family, and aging from late Qing to twenty-first century |date=2022 |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |isbn=978-1-009-06478-1 |edition= |location=Cambridge, United Kingdom |pages=37 |oclc=1292532755}}</ref>

[[File:OMBRE CHINOISE SERPENT.jpg|thumb|Zodiac snake, showing the ''shé'' (蛇) character for snake]] The '''snake''' ('''''') is the sixth of the twelve-year cycle of [[:en:Animal|Animal]]s which appear in the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]] related to the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. The '''Year of the Snake''' is associated with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Symbol|Symbol]] ''''''.<ref>[http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm ''Snake Horoscope Information''] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130217003643/http://www.usbridalguide.com/special/chinesehoroscopes/Snake.htm |date=2013-02-17 }} Retrieved 28 August 2012.</ref> Besides its use in the cycle of years, the zodiacal snake is otherwise used to also represent hours of the day. Snakes have a long and complicated place in Chinese mythology and culture.
==Other uses== The same twelve animals are also used to symbolize the cycle of hours in the day, each being associated with a two-hour time period. The hour of the snake is 9:00 to 11:00 a.m., the time when the [[:en:Sun|Sun]] warms up the Earth, and snakes are said to slither out of their holes. The month of the snake is the 4th month of the Chinese lunar calendar and it usually falls within the months of May through June depending on the Chinese to Gregorian calendar conversion. The reason the animal signs are referred to as [[:en:Zodiac|Zodiac]]al is that one's personality is said to be influenced by the animal signs ruling the time of birth, together with [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|elemental]] aspects of the animal signs within the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]. Similarly, the year governed by a particular animal sign is supposed to be characterized by it, with the effects particularly strong for people who were born in any year governed by the same animal sign.
==Symbology== In Chinese symbology, snakes are regarded as intelligent, with a tendency to lack scruples.<ref>Eberhard, ''sub'' "Snake (''She'')", p. 268.</ref>
==Origin myth== According to one [[:en:Chinese mythology|legend]], there is a reason for the order of the animals in the cycle. A race was held to cross a great river, and the order of the animals in the cycle was based upon their order in finishing the race. In this story, the [[:en:Snake|Snake]] compensated for not being the best swimmer by hitching a hidden ride on the [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse's]] hoof. When the horse was about to cross the finish line, the snake jumped out, scaring the horse, and thus edging it out for sixth place.
==Years and elements== {{See also|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} According to Chinese and related traditions, people born within the date ranges of the snake year have been said to have been born in the "Year of the Snake". Each snake year may traditionally be said to correspond with a cycle of five changes of [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)]], often translated as "elements". The following table lists dates for years of the snake together with the associated elemental signs: {| class="wikitable" |- ! Start date !! End date !!
